summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #289: the framework-agnostic DatePicker state machine (composite of text input + Calendar in a popover), covering open/close, date selection, input parse/validation, value↔calendar sync, and ARIA/data attrs. Adapters were explicitly out of scope.
summary.outcome: PR #697 opened with all 22 CI checks green. Delivered the full DatePicker agnostic core (116 tests, 100% function / 99.85% line coverage), fixed ~10 spec compile-blockers in code and spec, exposed the existing private `date_order` from ars-i18n so both DatePicker and DateField use CLDR-accurate locale ordering, fixed the xtask snapshot-count gate that silently skipped the entire date-time and data-display categories, and addressed all 8 Codex review findings.

repo: Spec §1.7 as written had ~10 compile-blockers vs. the real codebase: no `type Effect`, string effect names + `use_platform_effects()` closures instead of `PendingEffect::named`, `CalendarDate` field access (fields are pub(crate)), `nzu8` helper doesn't exist, no `PartialOrd`/`Ord` on `CalendarDate` (use `.compare() -> Ordering`), `ids.base_id` (accessor is `.id()`), `fn` instead of `IsDateUnavailableFn` (a `Callback` type), `Service::new` takes refs not values, `send` is `#[must_use]`
Effect convention is a typed enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`; focus is adapter-driven, never `use_platform_effects()` in core — Popover/Dialog are the canonical precedent

ars-i18n already had a complete private `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` (CLDR via ICU4X, JS Intl on wasm, (language,region) fallback) — both DateField and DatePicker had independently reimplemented only the fallback heuristic; the fix was to expose the existing function, not add a new trait method

DateField's `segment_order_for_locale` used a hand-rolled (language, region) match with DMY as the rest-of-world default; the correct default is DMY not MDY — consistent with DateField but the original DatePicker implementation used MDY as default

The snapshot-count gate (`xtask/src/lint.rs`) silently skipped two entire component categories (date-time, data-display) because `is_component_category` matched hyphenated names while source dirs and snapshot file segments use underscores
Context should derive only `Clone` + manual `Debug` when it holds `MessageFn` fields — `MessageFn` is not `Debug`. Calendar sets this precedent; spec §1.7 incorrectly derived `PartialEq` on Context

Controlled-open model follows the Popover precedent: state is committed on user events, `Effect::OpenChange` is emitted as a named marker, and the adapter fires the `on_open_change` callback so the parent can reconcile

Snapshot-reviewed label is read live by the CI gate via `gh pr view --json labels`, but `ci.yml`'s `pull_request:` trigger has no explicit `types:` so it defaults to `[opened, synchronize, reopened]` — not `labeled`. Adding the label after push never re-triggers the gate; a manual re-run is required

Proptest is better than unit tests at finding Bindable-state divergence: a redundant `open` Bindable that could diverge from `State` on controlled→uncontrolled prop flips was only caught by proptest, not by the unit suite
`Callback::new_ref` is the constructor for higher-ranked `for<'a> Fn(&'a T)` predicates (e.g. `IsDateUnavailableFn`); plain `Callback::new` fails type inference for this form
`ars-components` depends on `ars-i18n` with `default-features = false`; the CLDR path is only active under `--features i18n`, but both paths agree for canonical locales so tests remain deterministic

code.finding: Private `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` already existed with CLDR (icu4x probe date), JS Intl (wasm), and (language,region) fallback paths — was just not pub. Exposing it removes the need for per-component heuristic reimplementations.

`is_component_category` matched hyphenated category names (e.g. `"date-time"`) but source dirs and snapshot path segments use underscores (`date_time`). Fix: check both forms. This caused the snapshot-count gate to silently pass for the entire date-time and data-display categories.

CalendarDate: fields are `pub(crate)` not public; accessors are `year()->i32`, `month()->u8`, `day()->u8` (plain ints, no NonZero); `new_gregorian(i32,u8,u8)->Result<…>`; derives are Clone/Debug/PartialEq/Eq/Hash — no PartialOrd/Ord, so comparisons require `.compare(other) -> Ordering`

`is_date_unavailable` is `Option<IsDateUnavailableFn>` (= `Callback<dyn Fn(&CalendarDate)->bool+Send+Sync>`), not `Option<fn(&CalendarDate)->bool>`. Effect is a typed enum. `sync_props_into_ctx` + `Bindable::sync_controlled` is the canonical SyncProps pattern.
`on_trigger_keydown` returns `bool` to signal whether the event was handled (preventing double-toggle from keydown + native click). DatePicker's handler should follow this convention.

workflow: TDD discipline: write tests first to establish a failing RED baseline before implementing; proptest runs after unit tests and can catch invariant violations that unit tests miss
Explore agents are valuable for mapping existing impl patterns but should NOT be trusted for API specifics of the component being implemented — the spec and real sources are authoritative
Post-implementation audit runs 3 phases: spec/impl drift scan, iterative completeness check (repeat until dry), and coverage audit with a no-regression requirement
`cargo insta test` creates pending `.snap.new` files on first run; inspect them before `cargo insta accept` to validate semantic correctness, not just compilation
Pre-push gate is `cargo xci-fast` (fmt/check/clippy/unit/integration/spec-compile-snippets/snapshot-count/etc.); run before staging commits
When a stale staged change from a prior session breaks xci-fast, it must be fixed (not bypassed) before committing new work
Multi-commit history (logical units: drive-by fixes → foundation expose → tooling fix → main deliverable) makes PRs more reviewable than a single squashed commit
When CI has a structural race (label added after push, gate ran before label), the fix is a targeted manual re-run of that one job — not a new commit or force push

friction: Spec §1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers requiring extensive grep/read verification of the real codebase before writing any code — significant upfront investigation cost
Explore agents guessed the DatePicker API (invented event names, field names, part names) instead of deferring to the spec; their output had to be discarded and the spec read directly

Proptest found a real design flaw (redundant `open` Bindable diverging from `State` on controlled→uncontrolled prop flips) that required removing the field entirely and refactoring the transition arms
First proptest invariant written was itself incorrect (controlled-open coupling doesn't survive repeated same-value prop changes), requiring two iterations to arrive at correct invariants
Snapshot-reviewed label CI race: the gate always runs before the label is added (structural ordering), leaving a stale red check that requires a manual job re-run on every snapshot PR
Stale staged `pagination/mod.rs` change from a prior session broke xci-fast — had to fix it before the real commit could land
Session limit hit mid-deliberation on the `date_order` design decision, interrupting the reasoning flow
icu4x compilation is slow; background clippy runs across feature combos added significant wall-clock time
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` would fail due to pre-existing `utility/highlight` orphan snapshots — bare invocation is not usable without scoping to date_picker
open_items: CI structural race: snapshot-reviewed label gate runs before label can be added; options are (a) add `labeled`/`unlabeled` to `pull_request` trigger types (expensive — re-runs Coverage/Release on every label change) or (b) require a CHANGELOG entry instead of a label so it lands in the push
Pre-existing `utility/highlight` orphan snapshots block bare `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`; must scope to specific package or feature flag

DatePicker adapter implementations (Leptos, Dioxus) are separate tasks — adapters wire the named effects (FocusCalendar, RestoreFocusToTrigger, RestoreFocusToInput, OpenChange) to actual DOM focus and callback invocation
Presets (Today/Yesterday) were mentioned in the issue's test list but are explicitly excluded by spec §6.2/§6.4; if ever needed they require a spec change first

`IntlBackend` trait has no `date_order()` method — the CLDR/Intl path is accessed by calling the now-public `ars_i18n::date_order()` directly; a future backend needing to override ordering would have no hook
Pre-existing crate-wide `cargo doc` warnings (94 total, none from date_picker); doc hygiene is a separate cleanup task
Codex review was not completed (loop cancelled at user request); PR #697 is awaiting Codex's next pass
